items.title: Il lusso ancora nel mirino della Procura di Milano: coinvolti altri nove marchi
items.summary: Italian trade press reports the Milan Prosecutor's labour-exploitation (caporalato) probe has now extended document requests to nine more fashion houses (Chanel, Brunello Cucinelli, Bulgari, Etro, Moncler and others), continuing from the earlier round that named Gucci among 13 groups whose supply chains showed exploited Chinese workshop labour; Gucci itself is not newly implicated in this second wave but remains under the broader inquiry's document requests from the prior round. Critical-angle story on Italian luxury manufacturing practices running alongside Gucci's marketing narrative.

items.title: Is Gucci closing its Avenue Montaigne boutique in Paris?
items.summary: Gucci is reportedly on the verge of exiting its historic 1,500-square-metre Avenue Montaigne flagship in Paris amid an unresolved rent dispute with the landlord family, with the lease expiring at year-end and neither Gucci nor Kering confirming renewal; a replacement flagship at Rue Saint-Honore/Rue Castiglione is said to be lined up. Frames continued footprint rationalisation in a core European luxury market.

items.title: Gucci Beauty License Shift to L'Oreal: Analysts Weigh Impact on Coty's Future
items.summary: Sell-side analysts (Jefferies, Barclays) assess the accelerated transfer of the Gucci Beauty licence from Coty to L'Oreal, a year ahead of the original 2028 schedule; framed as a structural bright spot for Gucci/Kering's fragrance and beauty ambitions even as it strips a major franchise from licensing partner Coty. Continues to be cited in this week's European trade coverage of Kering's turnaround narrative.

items.title: Caporalato: 13 brands in fashion investigations, carabinieri from D&G, Prada, Gucci and Versace
items.summary: Continuing coverage of the Milan Prosecutor's labour-exploitation probe into 13 luxury groups' supply chains, including Gucci, where 12 workers were found in exploitative conditions at a subcontractor; carabinieri have requested governance and supplier-audit documentation. Gucci is not formally under investigation but remains named in the ongoing inquiry that Italian financial press keeps tracking this month.

items.title: Gucci en recul dans Brand Finance Italy 2026: ce que la baisse de valeur raconte du luxe italien
items.summary: Analysis of Gucci's slide in the Brand Finance Italy 100 2026 ranking (from third to fifth place), noting a double-digit brand-value reduction tied to a weakened Brand Strength Index score; interprets the drop as a signal of pricing-power and exclusivity concerns rather than pure sales-volume weakness. Included as background context still actively cited in July commentary on Gucci's positioning, though outside the 72-hour window.

items.title: Gucci, crisi di un modello
items.summary: Italian cultural-economic commentary arguing Kering became structurally over-dependent on Gucci and that the brand's growth-through-distribution-expansion model has hit its limits; contends efficiency measures cannot simply be applied to a desirability-driven business, and that Gucci's identity crisis is as much a design/positioning problem as a financial one. A critical-angle op-ed distinct from routine earnings recaps.

items.title: Perche il titolo Kering crolla oggi in Borsa?
items.summary: Market report on a Kering share slide of roughly 5.4 percent, with Citi and Barclays trimming price targets and analyst consensus leaning cautious (17 hold, 3 sell, 4 buy); attributes pressure to Gucci's eleventh consecutive quarterly revenue decline plus sector-wide softness from weak Chinese demand and Middle East tourism disruption. Retained as the most recent detailed market-reaction piece still referenced in ongoing coverage of Kering's stock trajectory.

items.title: Forum thread: Balenciaga or Gucci?
items.summary: Active PurseForum comparison thread where members weigh Balenciaga against Gucci on construction and value; several posters describe Gucci quality as having slipped ('their quality is poor and the design so matronly'), with one noting the brand 'aint what it used to be' without Tom Ford, while others defend current pieces. Community-tier read on collector sentiment toward Gucci's craftsmanship reputation.

items.title: Forum members review Demna's Fall 2026 runway debut as Gucci creative director
items.summary: Independent fashion-forum roundup of member reactions to Demna's debut Gucci runway show, spanning praise for a 'more consistent' brand identity to criticism that shoes and bags looked 'uninteresting' and parts of the collection 'vulgar and not sexy'; reflects the polarized, still-active community debate over whether Demna's direction fits Gucci's heritage.

items.title: Gucci, Emily Ratajkowski e Kate Moss: la campagna che divide e conquista il web
items.summary: Roundup of social-media reaction to the 'Beauty and the Bag' campaign featuring Kate Moss and Emily Ratajkowski nearly nude, covered only by handbags; reaction on Instagram/X and comment sections split between praise for a bold, on-brand creative statement and accusations of relying on cheap provocation rather than product storytelling. Included as a still-cited reference point for how Gucci's ambassador-driven campaigns land with the public, despite being outside the 14-day window; flagged as older background, weighted down accordingly.

items.title: Gucci Instagram: Monte Carlo summer campaign continuation
items.summary: Official Gucci-owned Instagram post continuing the Monte Carlo 2026 summer campaign tied to the Flora motif's 60th anniversary; flagged per objectivity policy as brand-owned activation content rather than independent public opinion. Engagement counts not extractable from the fetched view.

items.title: Kering Revenues Declined 6.2% in Q1, with Gucci Still Sluggish
items.summary: Financial-desk coverage reiterating Gucci's 14.3 percent reported Q1 revenue decline to EUR1.35 billion, with Western Europe and China cited as the weakest geographies even as North America improved 7 percent; the figure remains the baseline European trade press uses when assessing whether the L'Oreal beauty deal and De Meo's cost-cutting plan are moving the needle.
